risks: No Log Analytics workspace resource defined — ACA managed environment requires one for deployment (mandatory per Azure docs and IaC rules)
No managed identity on the Container App — WAF security best practice and IaC deployment rules require a user-assigned managed identity
No Event Grid subscription resource to route events from the topic to the Container App — the pipeline has no wiring between ingestion and processing
Cosmos DB is single-region with no zone redundancy (isZoneRedundant: false) — reduces reliability for production workloads
No network isolation — Cosmos DB, Event Grid, and ACA lack private endpoints or VNet integration
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Application Insights, alerts) — limits operational visibility
External ingress enabled on a backend event processor Container App — may expose unnecessary attack surface
correctionsRecommended: Add a Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces resource and connect it to the managed environment via logAnalyticsConfiguration (customerId + sharedKey)
Add a user-assigned managed identity (Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities) and attach it to the Container App
Add a Microsoft.EventGrid/eventSubscriptions resource to wire the Event Grid topic to the Container App endpoint
Consider enabling zone redundancy on both Cosmos DB locations and the ACA managed environment for improved reliability
Add private endpoints for Cosmos DB and disable key-based authentication in favor of RBAC
Add a CORS policy to the Container App ingress configuration per IaC deployment rules
Add Dapr component definitions for the Event Grid pub/sub binding and Cosmos DB state/output binding
Add resource tags for environment, project, and cost-center to support operational excellence
hardDependencies: Log Analytics workspace is required for ACA managed environment deployment — environment will fail to provision without logAnalyticsConfiguration
